reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome BEFORE building — the real ONNX graph removed added Gaussian noise and moved the image toward the clean reference, measured; then the full demo was driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver across all 5 pages; desktop+mobile 0px overflow measured), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21, WASM, fresh profile) BEFORE building. The real SCUNet ONNX graph (Heliosoph/scunet-onnx, scunet_color_real_psnr.onnx + .onnx.data) was loaded via ort.InferenceSession.create with externalData:[{path:'scunet_color_real_psnr.onnx.data', data}]. inputNames=['image'], outputNames=['denoised']. On an image with added Gaussian noise σ=25 the high-frequency (Laplacian) energy fell 0.276 -> 0.069 AND the RMSE-to-clean dropped 0.090 -> 0.048 — real denoising that moves TOWARD the clean reference, NOT a flat blur (RMSE would rise, not fall, if it just smoothed). Then the deployed demo ran end-to-end: Denoise produced real output at inference 4292 ms + composite 3 ms (HF 0.198 -> 0.058).
rubric.notes: Inference AND the dense RGBA composite run off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; the denoised ImageBitmap + noise-removed map are transferred (not cloned) back.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a denoised image [1,3,H,W] in [0,1], same spatial size as the (multiple-of-64-rounded) input. The worker clamps to RGBA and derives a 'noise-removed map' = amplified per-pixel |out-in|. Semantic sanity: HF energy falls toward a clean image (not to zero — real edges survive), and the noise map glows in noisy flats while easing off along strong edges — evidence it separates noise from structure, i.e. a denoiser not a blur.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT capability vs every built vision demo: image denoising (removing sensor/ISO/Gaussian noise). Not built before; distinct from deblur (motion/defocus), dehaze (atmosphere), low-light (exposure), super-res (resolution) and inpaint (holes).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Heliosoph/scunet-onnx, linked) + the specific variant shipped (scunet_color_real_psnr — blind real-world PSNR, the faithful default, not the texture-inventing GAN variant), the task (image-to-image / denoising), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no SCUNet class/task), the real I/O tensor names + shapes (image / denoised [1,3,H,W]), quantisation (fp32 + external-data sidecar), backend (WASM), download (~75 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API surface.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (the isolated per-worker ORT-web escape hatch, like nafnet/iat/lama/microdehaze/uvdoc) — shared lib/webai.js is untouched.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Heliosoph/scunet-onnx', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:75}). Both files are fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (cache name scunet-denoise-onnx-cache) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/Heliosoph/scunet-onnx/' and a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~75 MB). Verified in headless Chrome: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it downloaded and flipped the loader to ready. A per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: Never silently re-downloads the ~75 MB model; combined progress across the two files streams to the loader.

rubric.evidence: Loading/progress/ready/error states are announced in role=status aria-live regions (shared loader + page #status). The Denoise status warns it is a heavy model (several seconds @320 px). Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Denoising failed: …'); the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path. No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere (conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds).
rubric.notes: Measured 0 console errors and 0 network failures on load AND run across all 5 pages.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the noise-σ range slider (live-updating the preview), the accessible before/after reveal range (aria-valuetext '% denoised'), the sample gallery (rights-safe thumbnails as labelled buttons), the upload/drop dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space, drag+drop), the Denoise button, and a Download-cleaned anchor. Ladder pages add: wild's σ-blizzard push with a verdict; multi-model's Real-ESRGAN chain.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built nafnet/iat/microdehaze image-to-image demos; the reveal wipe uses a native range input for full keyboard support.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real SCUNet ONNX graph and the displayed image + HF numbers + noise-removed map are all derived from the real 'denoised' tensor. The synthetic Gaussian noise is applied transparently on the client (the user chooses σ and watches it removed, exactly like nafnet's synthetic blur / dehaze's synthetic haze); uploaded photos are treated as already-noisy and passed straight through with no added noise.
rubric.notes: Honest by construction: 'Add noise σ' is disclosed in every label; a real noisy upload skips it entirely. The blind variant stays faithful (does not invent texture).

rubric.evidence: All canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-labels (noisy input / denoised output / noise-removed map); every button has an accessible name (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the loader status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext. Light+dark inherit the WCAG-AA design system. Gallery thumbnails are real buttons with aria-pressed + license attribution on selection.
rubric.notes: Consulted modern-web-guidance for accessible live-region status + exposing canvas content; applied.

rubric.evidence: Mobile+desktop MATRIX measured 0 px horizontal overflow on ALL 5 pages at mobile (360×740, DPR3) AND desktop (1280×800). The two-col layout is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; the param-grid is auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases are inline-size:100%; readout wraps. scripts/responsive-check on the index reported desktop clean · mobile clean.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile cost (~75 MB fp32 download + a heavy compute) surfaced in at-a-glance; the default processing size is a modest 320 px to keep it usable on constrained devices.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense-output canvas composite run in the Web Worker; the denoised ImageBitmap + noise-removed map are transferred, not cloned (invariant 15's dense-output-off-main-thread rule). The main thread only draws bounded fit-canvases + the reveal composite. SCUNet is a heavy swin-conv U-Net (~4-7 s @320 px on single-thread WASM) — because it runs off-thread the UI never blocks and INP stays low despite the long compute.
rubric.notes: Default maxSide 320 balances quality vs the heavy compute; the synthetic-noise pass is a bounded per-pixel loop on a ≤512-px working copy, redrawn only on slider input.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy long-running image inference off main thread web worker so the UI never blocks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks; break up / offload heavy synchronous processing.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — SCUNet is a multi-second model, so ALL inference AND the dense composite run in a dedicated module Web Worker; the denoised ImageBitmap + noise map are transferred back (zero-copy). The main thread stays responsive throughout the long compute.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([denoisedBmp, noiseBmp]); UI responsive during the ~4-7 s denoise; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two column control panel with canvas image output and before/after reveal without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing, logical properties and flexible tracks (fr/minmax) before fixed widths; content-first flexbox with flex-wrap; use min-inline-size:0 so flex children can shrink — fewer media queries, no overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — two-col is flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0; param-grid is grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,220px),1fr); reveal/canvases inline-size:100% block-size:auto; logical properties throughout.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px horizontal overflow measured on all 5 pages at mobile 360×740 DPR3 AND desktop 1280×800

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for a long-running model; expose canvas-rendered content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live (and the status warns the compute is heavy); canvases carry role=img + aria-label; the reveal range exposes aria-valuetext; every button has a name.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; canvas aria-labels present

openQuestions: SCUNet is a heavy model (~4-7 s @320 px single-thread WASM). A WebGPU EP path or a smaller/quantised export would cut latency; the bundle also ships σ15/σ25/σ50 + gray variants that a future version could expose as a quality/speed selector.
The GAN variant (scunet_color_real_gan) invents sharper texture; a toggle between the faithful PSNR variant (shipped) and the GAN variant would be an honest, informative addition.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a variant selector (PSNR vs GAN, and matched-σ 15/25/50) so users can trade fidelity for sharpness / match a known noise level — additive on the same page.
If a WebGPU execution-provider path for onnxruntime-web becomes viable on GitHub Pages, add an opt-in GPU backend to cut SCUNet's multi-second latency.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for scunet-image-denoising (Heliosoph/scunet-onnx, scunet_color_real_psnr, image-to-image image denoising, fp32 + external-data sidecar, ~75 MB — a DISTINCT restoration capability not previously built). Built as the second demo of this wave alongside uvdoc-document-dewarping (the sanctioned dewarp substitute), because the requested shadow-removal target has no browser-runnable ONNX and was recorded status:blocked; none of harmonization/deraining/retouching have a runnable ONNX either, so denoising is the second distinct runnable restoration capability. RUNNABILITY VERIFIED FIRST in headless Chrome: the real ONNX graph removed added Gaussian noise (σ=25) — HF energy 0.276 -> 0.069, RMSE-to-clean 0.090 -> 0.048, moving toward the clean reference, not a blur. transformers.js has no SCUNet class, so it runs the ONNX directly via a per-worker onnxruntime-web pin. Full demo driven end-to-end via a headless-Chrome CDP page-driver: Download ~75 MB -> auto-init ready -> real output on the index + all 4 ladder pages (basics/practical clean + PNG download; wild σ-blizzard breaking point; multi-model chained Real-ESRGAN 4x, denoise-then-upscale vs naive). responsive-check 0 px overflow on all 5 pages at desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, 0 console errors, 0 network failures. SCUNet is a heavy model (~4-7 s @320 px WASM), disclosed in at-a-glance + run status; default processing size 320 px keeps it usable. License Apache-2.0.
